pattern: Group cells by their diagonal index (row minus column), sort each group independently, then write sorted values back into the matrix.
hints: All cells on the same top-left-to-bottom-right diagonal share the same value of (row - col). Use that difference as a dictionary key to bucket cells together.
Collect values for each diagonal, sort them, then scatter them back in top-to-bottom order within the diagonal.
Iterate over each unique (row - col) key, sort its values, and overwrite cells along the diagonal from the topmost cell downward.

edge_inputs.description: 1x3 row matrix — one diagonal per cell
already sorted matrix — output equals input
non-square matrix with more columns than rows

stress.description: random matrix values in [1,100], near-max dimensions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(size, 100))
    n = max(1, rng.randint(1, 100))
    mat = [[rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [mat]
